behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the file/env merge -> validation -> error-format boundary, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no network/subprocess/OS primitive is required beyond normal in-process wiring. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing either source can miss precedence/absence bugs across modules. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on missing-key reporting ("absent from both the file and the environment"), not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config file missing `database.host`, clear `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST`, and assert startup fails with an actionable message containing exact path `database.host`. This requires real wiring of config-file reader, env overlay, validator, and error renderer.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at env reading + path resolution + loader path selection boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required; a temp directory and process env are sufficient. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is path choice logic ("resolves ... relative to that directory") rather than OS-dependent watcher/event/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, place config there, ensure `~/.config/tool/config.yaml` is absent, run loader, and assert it loads from `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is specifically cross-source precedence (file vs env), so single-function happy-path tests are insufficient. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local process env and file read. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is deterministic override policy ("environment variable value is used"), not external infrastructure or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Create a config file with a key (e.g., `database.host: filehost`), set conflicting `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`, run loader, and assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure can occur at YAML parser exception mapping into CLI error output. A mocked parser error can hide incompatibilities in real exception fields. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network required; local malformed file plus real parser is enough. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text targets parser/formatter integration ("includes the line number") rather than broader OS-level runtime behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML with a known bad line in temp config, invoke loader, assert failure message includes the parser-reported line number. Use the real YAML library used in production.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on real OS filesystem primitives (directory creation + file write ordering at startup). MOCK-HIDE: fake FS/mocks can pass while real path creation/permissions/atomicity fail. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, real OS behavior is part of the failure boundary, so not Integration; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text requiring actual side effects ("creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding").
behaviors.test_description: Run CLI startup in a real temp HOME/XDG path where config dir is absent; assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline content, and loader continues successfully afterward.
